per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, any changes in your skin — like sweating or flushing?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, nothing out of the ordinary. I might get a little warm or feel some discomfort in my arms or legs during an episode, but nothing that really stands out as unusual. (pauses) Wait, actually... sometimes when it gets bad enough, I'll break out in a sweat, like my body's just trying to cool down or something. But like I said, it's not anything too different from how I feel normally - just a little worse, you know?

per_turn_leaks.student_message: And your stomach — any queasiness or nausea during these moments?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yeah... when the episodes are really bad, I'll get a little nauseous. It's not like I'm going to throw up or anything, but my stomach will just feel... off, you know? Like it's upset about something. But like with the sweating, it's not always there - only during the worse episodes.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: What about other parts of your body? Any pain, tingling, or unusual sensations?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Well, to be honest, there is one thing that happens sometimes when these episodes get really bad. My left arm will start to feel a little numb or tingly, like it's falling asleep or something. It's usually just in the arm, and it goes away once the episode passes, but it can be a bit unsettling.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Now let's zoom out. Any swelling you've noticed anywhere on your body?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah... I have noticed that my feet feel a bit swollen lately. My shoes are feeling tighter than usual, and when I take them off at night, they look like they're puffed up. It's not too bad, but it's definitely noticeable.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Let me ask about your medical history. What conditions have you been treated for?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Well, aside from the chest tightness and shortness of breath we've already talked about, I was diagnosed with high blood pressure about 8 years ago, and then type 2 diabetes around 5 years ago. And let's see... my doctor also told me that my cholesterol is a bit high, but I stopped taking the medication for it about a year ago because I thought I could manage it on my own. Other than that, just the usual stuff - nothing too out of the ordinary.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Now family history. Any medical problems run in your family?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Well, my father actually had a heart attack at a relatively young age... 52 to be exact. Unfortunately, he didn't make it. But aside from that, I think my family's got some other issues - my mom has hypertension and type 2 diabetes just like me, but she's doing okay for her age.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious or chronic conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My father had a heart attack at 52, which is pretty concerning considering I'm 54 now. My mom has hypertension and type 2 diabetes, but she's doing okay for her age - she's actually still alive at 78.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: We've covered a lot. Is there anything we haven't discussed that you think is relevant?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, I do have one more thing to add. My doctor told me my cholesterol was high about a year ago when I had my last checkup, and they prescribed me atorvastatin to try and bring it down. But for some reason, I just never got around to filling the prescription or taking the medication consistently. Maybe that's relevant?
